incorrects.description: Student selected 4 — counted symbols instead of total value

steps.text: You counted the symbols. The key tells us each symbol equals 2 students. Count by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 — miscounted symbols or misread another category

steps.text: Look at the Bus row. Count the symbols: 1, 2, 3, 4. Each symbol equals 2 students, so 4 times 2 equals 8.

incorrects.description: Student selected 6 — read Walk category or made calculation error

steps.text: Check which row the question asks about. We need Bus, not Walk. Count the Bus symbols and multiply by 2.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: System highlights Bus row. Each symbol pulses as counted: 1, 2, 3, 4 symbols. Then shows multiplication: 4 × 2 = 8 students.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. First, count the symbols in the Bus row: 1, 2, 3, 4 symbols. The key says each symbol equals 2 students. So 4 symbols times 2 equals 8 students. The answer is 8.
